attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place two watches on the TV stand.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

1. Objects and their types:
   - The scene contains a TV stand (a dark brown wooden cabinet with two visible drawers) that functions as a receptacle.
   - A large flat-screen TV is placed on top of the TV stand. The TV is not a receptacle and is considered an object.
   - There are three remote controls on a glass-top dining table. These are objects (not receptacles).
   - A small red rectangular object (possibly a card or tag) is also on the dining table. It is an object.
   - Four wicker-backed chairs surround the dining table. These are not receptacles and are not relevant to the domain types.
   - Two cardboard boxes are on the dining table. These are objects but are not defined as receptacles in the domain file and are not relevant to the task.
   - The floor is wooden and the walls are light beige; these are not objects in the domain.

2. Quantities of each object type:
   - Receptacles: 1 (the TV stand).
   - Objects: 1 TV, 3 remote controls, 1 red rectangular object, 2 cardboard boxes, 4 chairs (though chairs are not relevant to the domain predicates).
   - No knives, microwaves, fridges, or sinks are visible in the scene.

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The TV is on top of the TV stand (inReceptacle TV TV_stand is not applicable because TV is not in the TV stand; rather, TV is placed on top of it — but since “on” is not a defined predicate for receptacle containment, we note that TV is not in the TV stand).
   - The three remote controls, red object, and cardboard boxes are on the glass-top dining table.
   - The dining table is surrounded by four chairs.
   - The TV stand is positioned against a wall, separate from the dining area.

4. Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:
   - The TV stand is closed (not opened) and is not currently holding any objects relevant to the task (no watches are present).
   - The TV is not toggled on or off in the image (no visual indicator of power state).
   - The remote controls and red object are stationary on the table; none are held by an agent.
   - No objects are currently in the TV stand.
   - No objects are clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled (as no actions have been performed).

5. Colors, positions, and other visual properties:
   - The TV stand is dark brown wood with a flat top and two drawers; it is positioned against a wall, near the left side of the scene.
   - The TV is black with a thin frame, sitting centered on the TV stand.
   - The dining table has a transparent glass top and is surrounded by four light brown wicker chairs.
   - The three remote controls are silver with blue and red buttons; they are placed on the table — two near the bottom edge and one near the top.
   - The red rectangular object is small and bright red, located near the center of the table.
   - The cardboard boxes are open and brown, placed near the top of the table.
   - The floor is medium-brown hardwood with visible grain; the walls are light beige.

Note: The instruction mentions “two watches,” but no watches are visible in any of the images. Therefore, the task cannot be completed with the current objects present in the scene. The robot would need to locate or acquire watches before placing them on the TV stand.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem place_watches_on_tv_stand)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        tv_stand - receptacle
        tv - object
        remote1 remote2 remote3 - object
        red_object - object
        box1 box2 - object
        chair1 chair2 chair3 chair4 - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable tv_stand)
        (not (opened tv_stand))
        (atLocation agent1 tv_stand) ; Assuming agent starts at TV stand for simplicity, as no initial location is specified
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean remote1))
        (not (isClean remote2))
        (not (isClean remote3))
        (not (isClean red_object))
        (not (isHot remote1))
        (not (isHot remote2))
        (not (isHot remote3))
        (not (isHot red_object))
        (not (isCool remote1))
        (not (isCool remote2))
        (not (isCool remote3))
        (not (isCool red_object))
        (not (isOn tv))
        (not (isToggled tv))
        (not (isSliced remote1))
        (not (isSliced remote2))
        (not (isSliced remote3))
        (not (isSliced red_object))
        ; No watches are present in the scene, so we cannot initialize them
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; The goal is to place two watches on the TV stand.
            ; However, no watches are present in the scene.
            ; Since watches are not defined in the objects and cannot be acquired from the scene,
            ; this goal is unachievable with the current objects.
            ; For the sake of PDDL structure, we assume watches are available as objects.
            ; But note: In reality, this task cannot be completed as described.
            ; We will define two watch objects for the sake of the problem.
            ; This is an assumption to make the problem solvable in PDDL.
            ; In a real scenario, the robot would need to find or acquire watches first.
            ; Since the domain does not support acquiring new objects, we must define them here.
            ; This is a limitation of the current scene and domain.
        )
    )
)
```

**Note**: The instruction to "Place two watches on the TV stand" cannot be fulfilled with the objects present in the scene, as no watches are visible. The PDDL problem above includes a placeholder for the goal, but it is unachievable without defining the watches as objects and having a way to acquire them. Since the domain does not include actions for acquiring new objects, and the scene does not contain watches, the task is impossible as specified. The PDDL problem is structured to reflect the scene, but the goal cannot be achieved with the current objects.

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place two watches on the TV stand.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a dining area and a living area with a TV stand. The relevant objects for the task are:

- **TV Stand**: A dark brown wooden cabinet with two drawers, positioned against a wall. It is a receptacle as it can hold objects. It is currently empty. Its top surface is flat and clear, suitable for placing items.
- **Watches**: There are two watches on the glass-top dining table. Each watch is small, rectangular, with a dark casing and a blue digital display. They are positioned on the table surface, not inside any container. The watches are not defined as receptacles or any of the domain types (microwave, fridge, sink, knife), so they are classified as generic “object” types.
- **Dining Table**: A large rectangular table with a transparent glass top and wooden frame. It is not a receptacle in the domain sense. It currently holds the two watches and three open cardboard boxes. The boxes are empty and do not contain any relevant objects.
- **Chairs**: Four wicker chairs with wooden frames surround the dining table. They are not receptacles and are not involved in the task.
- **Cardboard Boxes**: Three open, empty boxes are on the table. They are not relevant to the task as they are not receptacles in the domain and do not hold the watches.
- **Red Object**: A small red rectangular object (possibly a card or tag) is on the table near the center. It is not relevant to the task.

The robot is not currently at any object’s location. The watches are not being held by any agent and are not in any receptacle. The TV stand is closed (not opened) and is not currently holding any objects. The robot must first navigate to the dining table to pick up a watch, then go to the TV stand to place it. The same sequence must be repeated for the second watch. The TV stand is not openable or toggleable, so no OpenObject or ToggleObject actions are needed for it. The watches do not need to be cleaned, heated, cooled, or sliced. The task requires only GotoLocation, PickupObject, and PutObjectInReceptacle actions.

The spatial relationships are:
- Both watches are on the dining table.
- The TV stand is in a different area, likely adjacent to the dining area, as seen in the second image.
- No objects are currently in the TV stand.
- The robot is not at any location initially.

Colors and positions:
- The TV stand is dark brown wood.
- The watches are dark gray/black with blue digital displays.
- The dining table is transparent glass with a light brown wooden frame.
- The floor is medium brown wood planks.
- The walls are light beige.

The task is to move the two watches from the dining table to the TV stand. The robot must perform this sequentially, picking up one watch at a time and placing it on the TV stand.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem place_watches_on_tv_stand)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        tv_stand - receptacle
        watch1 watch2 - object
        dining_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation robot tv_stand))
        (not (atLocation robot dining_table))
        (atLocation watch1 dining_table)
        (atLocation watch2 dining_table)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (inReceptacle watch1 tv_stand))
        (not (inReceptacle watch2 tv_stand))
        (not (opened tv_stand))
        (not (isOn tv_stand))
        (not (isClean watch1))
        (not (isClean watch2))
        (not (isHot watch1))
        (not (isHot watch2))
        (not (isCool watch1))
        (not (isCool watch2))
        (not (isSliced watch1))
        (not (isSliced watch2))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle watch1 tv_stand)
        (inReceptacle watch2 tv_stand)
    ))
)
```

    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.

    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

attempts.response:
The given PDDL domain `put_task` defines a limited set of object types (`microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, `receptacle`, and `object`) and actions that only operate on those types. The instruction “Place two watches on the TV stand” cannot be modeled under this domain for the following reasons:

1. **No “watch” object type exists** in the domain. The domain only recognizes `knife`, `receptacle`, and generic `object`, but “watch” is not among them.
2. **No “TV stand” is defined as a receptacle** in the domain. Although the TV stand is a physical surface that can hold objects, it is not declared as a `receptacle` type in the domain, so the `PutObjectInReceptacle` action cannot be applied to it.
3. **No objects matching domain types are present** in the scene. The visible objects (remote controls, cardboard boxes, TV, chairs, table) are not of type `microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, or `knife`, and the table/TV stand are not explicitly typed as `receptacle` in the domain.
4. **The domain lacks a generic “place on surface” action** — all placement is constrained to `receptacle` objects, which the TV stand is not.

Therefore, **it is impossible to generate a valid PDDL problem** under the given domain that fulfills the instruction “Place two watches on the TV stand,” because the required objects and receptacles do not exist in the domain’s type system, and no actions can be applied to non-domain objects.

---

However, to comply with the output format requirement, and assuming the task must be represented, we must either:

- Extend the domain (which we cannot do), or
- Introduce placeholder objects that map to domain types, even if semantically incorrect.

Since we are constrained to the given domain, and no valid mapping exists, the only logically consistent PDDL problem is one that declares **no valid actions can be performed** — i.e., the goal is unreachable under the domain.

But since the problem must be generated, we will create a minimal, syntactically correct PDDL problem that reflects the scene as closely as possible under domain constraints, using the only available object types and making minimal assumptions.

We will:

- Declare the agent.
- Declare the TV stand as a `receptacle` (even though not in domain — this is a necessary assumption to proceed).
- Declare “watch1” and “watch2” as generic `object`s.
- Assume the watches are initially on the table (since no other location is specified).
- Assume the table is also a `receptacle` (to allow picking up from it).
- Define the goal as putting both watches in the TV stand.

This is a **workaround** and **violates the strict domain constraints**, but it is the only way to produce a syntactically valid PDDL problem that attempts to model the instruction.

---

**PDDL Problem File:**

```lisp
(define (problem place_watches_on_tv_stand)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        tv_stand - receptacle
        dining_table - receptacle
        watch1 watch2 - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 dining_table)
        (inReceptacle watch1 dining_table)
        (inReceptacle watch2 dining_table)
        (openable tv_stand)
        (openable dining_table)
        (opened dining_table)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle watch1 tv_stand)
        (inReceptacle watch2 tv_stand)
    ))
)
```

---

**Important Note:**

This PDDL problem is **not valid under the original domain constraints** because:

- `tv_stand` and `dining_table` are not declared as `receptacle` types in the domain.
- `watch1` and `watch2` are not domain-defined objects.
- The domain does not support placing objects on non-receptacle surfaces.

However, given the requirement to generate a PDDL file, this is the closest possible representation under forced assumptions. In a real robotic planning system, this problem would fail validation against the domain.

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))

            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife

    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )
